author: w3Lb
content: Delivered in commit 2c74dc9. Shared process_one_file worker (lock/read/apply/backup/write, diagnostics returned not printed) used by file mode and per-op script passes; par_iter preserves input order so printing/undo/counters run on the main thread in discovery order — output byte-identical across thread counts (regression-tested). Side improvement: a failed write now clears the outcome's undo entry, so undo can't restore a write that never landed (the old sequential path recorded undo before writing). --threads N long-only (-j taken by --json), default all cores; --confirm stays sequential; script-mode lock scope narrowed to per-(op,file) pass, documented. Test gotcha worth remembering: determinism tests comparing stdout across runs must normalize temp-dir paths — first version failed on the tempdir name, not real nondeterminism. 6 new e2e tests. 23 suites green, clippy/fmt clean.
